query: Generate a production docker-compose.yml for a Python web application with database
example:
version: "3.9"

services:
  app:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      target: runtime
    image: app:${APP_VERSION:-latest}
    container_name: app
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      - ENVIRONMENT=production
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://appuser:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@db:5432/appdb
      - LOG_LEVEL=info
      - PORT=8000
    env_file:
      - .env.production
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 40s
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "0.50"
          memory: 256M
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.10"
          memory: 128M
    networks:
      - app-network
    logging:
      driver: json-file
      options:
        max-size: "10m"
        max-file: "3"
    read_only: false
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true

  db:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    container_name: db
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: appdb
      POSTGRES_USER: appuser
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U appuser -d appdb"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
    networks:
      - app-network
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true

volumes:
  postgres-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  app-network:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: 172.20.0.0/16
